Interface IController

<<<<<<< HEAD
All Known Implementing Classes:
Controller
======= >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1
public interface IController
Outlines the controller layer of the game, which wraps the sudoku package, facilitating all communication between the model and view layers of the MVC stack of the game. Stores the current state of the Sudoku game, which is mutated as the game progresses.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clearCell(int row, int col)
    Clears the target cell of the value which it currently stores.
    ISudokuGrid
    getGridState()
    Returns the current state of the Sudoku grid.
    void
    hint()
    Generates a new given cell in a random place on the Sudoku grid.
    void
    newGame(ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty difficulty)
    Generates a new sudoku grid, starting with the given difficulty.
    void
    placeValue(int row, int col, int value)
    Attempts to place a given value in the target cell.
    void
    reset()
    Resets the grid to an empty state.
    void
    solve()
    Completes the grid by filling in any blank cells, and replacing any that are incorrect.

  • Method Details

    • newGame

      void newGame(ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty difficulty)
      Generates a new sudoku grid, starting with the given difficulty.
      Parameters:
      difficulty - The difficulty, which determines the number of givens in the grid.
      See Also:
      • ISudokuGrid
      • ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty

    • placeValue

      void placeValue(int row, int col, int value) throws InvalidCellException, InvalidValueException, GivenCellException
      Attempts to place a given value in the target cell. <<<<<<< HEAD

      The value to be placed within the cell must fall within the bounds of the size of the =======

      The value to be placed within the cell must fall within the bounds of the size of the >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1 ISudokuGrid. For example, a typical grid has a size of 9, meaning the values 1-9 inclusive must be placed exactly once in each row, column, and box.

      Parameters:
      row - The row (or y-coordinate) of the target cell.
      col - The column (or x-coordinate) of the target cell.
      value - The value to attempt to place within the given cell.
      Throws:
      <<<<<<< HEAD
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      InvalidValueException - if the given value exceeds the size of the Sudoku grid.
      GivenCellException - if the given cell stores a given value: these cannot be overwritten.
      =======
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      InvalidValueException - if the given value exceeds the size of the Sudoku grid.
      GivenCellException - if the given cell stores a given value: these cannot be overwritten.
      >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1
      See Also:
      • ISudokuGrid

    • clearCell

      void clearCell(int row, int col) throws InvalidCellException, EmptyCellException, GivenCellException
      Clears the target cell of the value which it currently stores.
      Parameters:
      row - The row (or y-coordinate) of the target cell.
      col - The column (or x-coordinate) of the target cell.
      Throws:
      <<<<<<< HEAD
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      =======
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1
      EmptyCellException - if the given cell is already empty.
      GivenCellException - if trying to clear a given cell.

    • hint

      void hint() throws GridSolvedException
      Generates a new given cell in a random place on the Sudoku grid. <<<<<<< HEAD

      If all cells are filled, but the grid has not yet been solved, the algorithm will replace a cell with an existing, but incorrect, entry with a given value.

      The algorithm will never place a hint in a cell in which the player has placed a value which is already correct.

      Throws:
      GridSolvedException - if the grid has already been solved: there are no more cells left to hint.
      =======

      If all cells are filled, but the grid has not yet been solved, the algorithm will replace a cell with an existing, but incorrect, entry with a given value.

      The algorithm will never place a hint in a cell in which the player has placed a value which is already correct.

      Throws:
      GridSolvedException - if the grid has already been solved: there are no more cells left to hint.
      >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1

  • solve

    void solve() throws GridSolvedException
    Completes the grid by filling in any blank cells, and replacing any that are incorrect.
    Throws:
    GridSolvedException - if the grid has already been solved.

  • reset

    void reset() throws EmptyGridException
    Resets the grid to an empty state. <<<<<<< HEAD

    Note that given/hint cells are not cleared.

    =======

    Note that given/hint cells are not cleared.

    >>>>>>> origin/stable-m1
    Throws:
    EmptyGridException - if the grid is already empty.
